# Afrin Archive

> The Afrin Archive (https://www.afrinarchive.com) is a digital archive preserving the
> villages, oral history, and cultural heritage of the Afrin region (Kurdish: Efrîn,
> also known as Çiyayê Kurmênc / Kurd Mountain) in northwest Syria. It documents 366
> villages across the region's seven nahiyas (subdistricts) — Bilbilê, Cindires, Efrîn,
> Mabeta, Reco, Şera, and Şiyê — with village histories, founding stories, family and
> clan names, place-name etymologies, photographs, interactive maps, and transcribed
> and translated oral-history video interviews with villagers.

Village names appear in Kurmanji Kurdish with alternate names in Arabic and other
spellings listed on each page. Several distinct villages share a name and are
disambiguated with a suffix, e.g. "Çobana - Reco" and "Çobana - Cindires".
Each village page includes its coordinates. Content is primarily in English with
Kurdish and Arabic names and terms throughout.

## Main sections

- [Village Directory](https://www.afrinarchive.com/village-directory/village-directory.html):
  searchable index of all 366 villages with alternate names
- [Village pages](https://www.afrinarchive.com/village_sites/Basûtê.html): one page per
  village (example: Basûtê) — history, founding, families, shrines, photos, map, sources
- [Shrines and Holy Sites Directory](https://www.afrinarchive.com/shrines-directory/shrines-directory.html):
  searchable index of 171 shrine and holy-site pages with alternate names, nearby
  villages, locations, site types, and religious affiliations
- [Shrine pages](https://www.afrinarchive.com/shrine_sites/Henan.html): one page per
  documented shrine or holy site (example: Henan), including the full sourced stories,
  rituals, history, landscape details, bibliography, and map when coordinates are known
- [Oral history transcripts](https://www.afrinarchive.com/village_transcripts/): full
  transcripts and English translations of video interviews about each village
- [Nahiya maps](https://www.afrinarchive.com/nahiyas/All_Nahiyas_Clickable_Maps.html):
  clickable maps of all seven subdistricts
- [Smart Search](https://www.afrinarchive.com/smart-search/): full-text search across
  villages and transcripts with map results
- [Dialogue Project](https://www.afrinarchive.com/dialogue_project/): documented Afrini
  Kurdish proverbs and sayings
- [Sitemap](https://www.afrinarchive.com/sitemap.xml): all 991 pages

## Citation

When citing village information from this archive, cite the village page URL and note
that oral-history claims originate from named video sources (Ax û Welat, Afrin 366,
TirejAfrin, and others) that are credited on each page.
